methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Ideon Technologies is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Gary Agnew that has raised $30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $30M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $57M. Peer-median methodology examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B (median: $355M), implying $355M for the target. The peer sample spans fusion energy (Commonwealth at $5.0B), wildfire detection (Pano AI at $500M), geothermal (Sage Geosystems at $400M), and carbon capture and utilization (Reverion at $310M, CarbonCapture Inc. at $300M).

Triangulating these two approaches yields a valuation range of $48M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $206M. The wide dispersion reflects the heterogeneity within the Climate & Energy sector and stage; peer valuations span nearly two orders of magnitude. Key uncertainties include Ideon's technology maturity relative to higher-valued peers, capital efficiency versus peer benchmarks, and whether the company's specific climate solution commands premium or discount positioning within this cohort.
